title: Garlicky Tender Ethiopian Pork Knuckle Tagine
description: A keto-friendly, post-workout Ethiopian dish featuring pork knuckle in a flavorful nimono simmer.
instructions: Season the pork knuckle with salt, black pepper, and berbere spice. Let it sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork knuckle until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side. Remove the pork from the pot and set it aside.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, the onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken broth, thyme, and browned pork knuckle back to the pot. Cover and bring to a boil.
Transfer the pot to the oven and simmer, covered, at 300°F (150°C) for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Stir in the fresh cilantro.
Serve the pork knuckle with the juices spooned over the top.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Slice the pork knuckle into thick slices and serve hot with the juices spooned over the top.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
